oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Milk meets muscle: Practical dairy insights a beef practitioner can use tomorrow
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Barnhardt, Tera
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Dairy production systems operate under intensified manage­ment, frequent animal interaction and data-driven decision-making. While beef systems are typically more extensive, many principles from dairy medicine can be adapted to improve beef herd productivity, health and profitability. This paper outlines key areas where dairy practices – transition management, co­lostrum evaluation, standardized protocols, reproductive moni­toring, nutrition and economic awareness – can be translated into practical, high-return interventions in beef operations. The central thesis is that “cattle are cattle,” and that applying a higher-resolution management lens can sharpen outcomes in beef practice.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 30
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
